creator: pat@patmaddox.com
text:
I tested by widening the version in Makefile, and it works - so it doesn't seem like the patches to the other files are necessary.

I'm good with this. I have a couple questions:

1. Is it preferable to do a commit only to Makefile, since the other patches don't appear to be necessary?
2. How did you determine that the update to py-pathspec caused a break in py-dbt-core?

I'm specifically curious about Q2 so I can identify potentially breaking downstream ports as I maintain dependencies.
